prerequisites: An active Visma e-conomic agreement (account) whose data you want to read.
A Visma e-conomic developer agreement, used to register the app that produces the App Secret Token. A free developer agreement can be created through the e-conomic Developer Network.
A registered app in the developer agreement, from which you obtain both the App Secret Token and the app installation URL.
An agreement administrator who grants the app access to their e-conomic agreement, producing the Agreement Grant Token.

authMethods.name: App Secret Token + Agreement Grant Token (paired token headers)
authMethods.summary: The e-conomic REST API authenticates every request with two secret tokens sent as HTTP headers: X-AppSecretToken and X-AgreementGrantToken. The App Secret Token (the app_secret_token field) identifies your app and is your secret half of the combined key, generated when you register the app in your developer agreement. The Agreement Grant Token (the agreement_grant_token field) identifies the grant issued by an agreement that lets your app access that account's data, and is produced when an agreement administrator grants the app access through its installation URL. Both tokens must be supplied together. For read-only testing, e-conomic accepts the literal value 'demo' for both tokens, which permits GET requests only.

setupSteps.title: Register an app and get the App Secret Token
setupSteps.body: Log in to your e-conomic developer agreement and open the Apps section. Click 'New app', select an appropriate role, and create the app. The system generates an AppSecretToken, which is your developer half of the combined authentication key. This value is shown only once, so copy and store it securely. This becomes the app_secret_token credential.

setupSteps.title: Grant the app access to an agreement to get the Agreement Grant Token
setupSteps.body: From the app's tokens section, retrieve its installation URL and share it with an administrator of the e-conomic accounting agreement you want to read. The administrator first clicks 'Administer' on their agreement, then opens the installation URL and grants the app access. e-conomic then issues an AgreementGrantToken, the second half of the key that gives API access to that agreement's data. This becomes the agreement_grant_token credential.

setupSteps.title: Provide both tokens to the connector
setupSteps.body: Configure the connector with the two required secret fields: agreement_grant_token and app_secret_token. The connector sends them on every request as the X-AgreementGrantToken and X-AppSecretToken headers (with Content-Type: application/json) to the e-conomic REST API. To validate connectivity without live data you may set both fields to the value 'demo', which allows GET requests only.
